reports.query:
## Global Flight Network — Airport Network Analysis

This page analyzes the worldwide airport network built from the **OpenFlights** dataset (airports, airlines and routes) imported into Neo4j. Distance on all routes is calculated geographically using `point.distance()`. Find out the busiest hubs, how they are connected, the distribution of route distances and the location of the world's airports on the map.

reports.title: Airports
reports.query: MATCH (a:Airport) RETURN count(a) AS Airports

reports.title: Routes
reports.query: MATCH ()-[r:ROUTE]->() RETURN count(r) AS Routes

reports.title: Airlines
reports.query: MATCH (a:Airline) RETURN count(a) AS Airlines

reports.title: Countries
reports.query: MATCH (c:Country) RETURN count(c) AS Countries

reports.title: Top 15 Busiest Airports (by total routes)
reports.query:
MATCH (a:Airport) WHERE a.iata IS NOT NULL
WITH a, COUNT { (a)-[:ROUTE]->() } + COUNT { (a)<-[:ROUTE]-() } AS routes
RETURN a.iata + ' · ' + coalesce(a.city, a.name) AS Airport, routes AS Routes
ORDER BY Routes DESC LIMIT 15

reports.title: Connection Network of the 10 Largest Hubs
reports.query:
MATCH (a:Airport) WHERE a.iata IS NOT NULL
WITH a, COUNT { (a)-[:ROUTE]-() } AS deg ORDER BY deg DESC LIMIT 10
WITH collect(a) AS hubs
UNWIND hubs AS h
MATCH (h)-[r:ROUTE]->(b:Airport) WHERE b IN hubs
RETURN h, r, b

reports.title: Route Distance Distribution (500 km buckets)
reports.query:
MATCH ()-[r:ROUTE]->() WHERE r.distanceKm IS NOT NULL AND r.distanceKm <= 15000
WITH (r.distanceKm / 500) * 500 AS bucket, count(*) AS routes
RETURN bucket AS DistanceKm, routes AS Routes ORDER BY bucket

reports.title: Top 10 Airlines by Number of Routes
reports.query:
MATCH ()-[r:ROUTE]->()
WITH r.airline AS code, count(*) AS routes ORDER BY routes DESC LIMIT 10
OPTIONAL MATCH (al:Airline) WHERE al.iata = code
RETURN coalesce(al.name, code) AS Airline, routes AS Routes ORDER BY Routes DESC

reports.title: Airport Connectivity Ranking (downloadable CSV)
reports.query:
MATCH (a:Airport)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(co:Country) WHERE a.iata IS NOT NULL
WITH a, co, COUNT { (a)-[:ROUTE]->() } AS departures, COUNT { (a)<-[:ROUTE]-() } AS arrivals
RETURN a.iata AS Code, a.name AS Airport, coalesce(a.city,'') AS City, co.name AS Country,
       departures AS Departures, arrivals AS Arrivals, departures + arrivals AS Total
ORDER BY Total DESC LIMIT 200

reports.title: World Map of Major Airports (>50 routes)
reports.query:
MATCH (a:Airport) WHERE a.iata IS NOT NULL
WITH a, COUNT { (a)-[:ROUTE]-() } AS deg WHERE deg > 50
RETURN a

reports.title: Global Airport Density Heatmap
reports.query:
MATCH (a:Airport) WHERE a.iata IS NOT NULL
WITH a, COUNT { (a)-[:ROUTE]-() } AS deg WHERE deg > 0
RETURN a

## Country & Route Connectivity

Pick a **source** and a **destination** country below. The reports show direct air links, and — crucially — an **auto-built connecting itinerary** for any pair, even when no direct flight exists (e.g. Georgia → Japan). Dijkstra searches over geographic route distances between the top hubs of each country to find the shortest multi-stop path; the graph and the leg-by-leg table reveal every intermediate airport and airline. Tables are downloadable as CSV.

reports.title: Source country
reports.query:
MATCH (c:Country) WHERE toLower(c.name) CONTAINS toLower($input)
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS value, c.name AS display ORDER BY value LIMIT 10

reports.title: Destination country
reports.query:
MATCH (c:Country) WHERE toLower(c.name) CONTAINS toLower($input)
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS value, c.name AS display ORDER BY value LIMIT 10

reports.title: Direct routes (source → destination)
reports.query:
MATCH (a:Airport)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(:Country {name:$neodash_source_country})
MATCH (a)-[r:ROUTE]->(b:Airport)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(:Country {name:$neodash_destination_country})
RETURN count(r) AS DirectRoutes

reports.title: Shortest connecting distance (km)
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Airport)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(:Country {name:$neodash_source_country})
WITH s, COUNT { (s)-[:ROUTE]->() } AS sd WHERE sd > 0
WITH s ORDER BY sd DESC LIMIT 3
WITH collect(s) AS sources
MATCH (d:Airport)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(:Country {name:$neodash_destination_country})
WITH sources, d, COUNT { (d)<-[:ROUTE]-() } AS dd WHERE dd > 0
WITH sources, d ORDER BY dd DESC LIMIT 3
WITH sources, collect(d) AS dests
UNWIND sources AS src
UNWIND dests AS dst
CALL apoc.algo.dijkstra(src, dst, 'ROUTE>', 'distanceKm') YIELD weight
RETURN toInteger(weight) AS MinDistanceKm ORDER BY MinDistanceKm ASC LIMIT 1

reports.title: Top 15 Destination Countries from Source Country
reports.query:
MATCH (a:Airport)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(:Country {name:$neodash_source_country})
MATCH (a)-[:ROUTE]->(b:Airport)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(c:Country)
WHERE c.name <> $neodash_source_country
RETURN c.name AS Country, count(*) AS Routes ORDER BY Routes DESC LIMIT 15

reports.title: Direct Routes: Source → Destination (downloadable CSV)
reports.query:
MATCH (a:Airport)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(:Country {name:$neodash_source_country})
MATCH (a)-[r:ROUTE]->(b:Airport)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(:Country {name:$neodash_destination_country})
OPTIONAL MATCH (al:Airline) WHERE al.iata = r.airline
RETURN a.iata AS From, coalesce(a.city,'') AS FromCity, b.iata AS To, coalesce(b.city,'') AS ToCity,
       coalesce(al.name, r.airline) AS Airline, r.distanceKm AS DistanceKm
ORDER BY DistanceKm ASC

reports.title: Connecting Flight Path on the Map (stops numbered 1 → N)
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Airport)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(:Country {name:$neodash_source_country})
WITH s, COUNT { (s)-[:ROUTE]->() } AS sd WHERE sd > 0
WITH s ORDER BY sd DESC LIMIT 3
WITH collect(s) AS sources
MATCH (d:Airport)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(:Country {name:$neodash_destination_country})
WITH sources, d, COUNT { (d)<-[:ROUTE]-() } AS dd WHERE dd > 0
WITH sources, d ORDER BY dd DESC LIMIT 3
WITH sources, collect(d) AS dests
UNWIND sources AS src
UNWIND dests AS dst
CALL apoc.algo.dijkstra(src, dst, 'ROUTE>', 'distanceKm') YIELD path, weight
WITH path, weight ORDER BY weight ASC LIMIT 1
WITH nodes(path) AS ns, relationships(path) AS rs
WITH [i IN range(0, size(ns)-1) | apoc.create.vNode(['Airport'], {
       iata: toString(i+1) + ' · ' + ns[i].iata,
       latitude: ns[i].latitude, longitude: ns[i].longitude, location: ns[i].location,
       stop: i+1, name: coalesce(ns[i].city, ns[i].name)})] AS vn, rs
UNWIND range(0, size(vn)-2) AS j
RETURN vn[j] AS a,
       apoc.create.vRelationship(vn[j], 'LEG', {seq: j+1, distanceKm: rs[j].distanceKm}, vn[j+1]) AS r,
       vn[j+1] AS b

reports.title: Airports of Source & Destination Countries on the Map
reports.query:
MATCH (a:Airport)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(c:Country)
WHERE c.name IN [$neodash_source_country, $neodash_destination_country] AND a.iata IS NOT NULL
RETURN a

reports.title: Connecting Itinerary, Leg by Leg (intermediate airports & airlines)
reports.query:
MATCH (s:Airport)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(:Country {name:$neodash_source_country})
WITH s, COUNT { (s)-[:ROUTE]->() } AS sd WHERE sd > 0
WITH s ORDER BY sd DESC LIMIT 3
WITH collect(s) AS sources
MATCH (d:Airport)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(:Country {name:$neodash_destination_country})
WITH sources, d, COUNT { (d)<-[:ROUTE]-() } AS dd WHERE dd > 0
WITH sources, d ORDER BY dd DESC LIMIT 3
WITH sources, collect(d) AS dests
UNWIND sources AS src
UNWIND dests AS dst
CALL apoc.algo.dijkstra(src, dst, 'ROUTE>', 'distanceKm') YIELD path, weight
WITH path, weight ORDER BY weight ASC LIMIT 1
UNWIND range(0, length(path)-1) AS i
WITH nodes(path)[i] AS a, nodes(path)[i+1] AS b, relationships(path)[i] AS r, i
CALL { WITH r OPTIONAL MATCH (al:Airline {iata:r.airline}) RETURN al.name AS alName ORDER BY alName LIMIT 1 }
RETURN i+1 AS Leg, a.iata AS From, coalesce(a.city, a.name) AS FromCity, b.iata AS To, coalesce(b.city, b.name) AS ToCity, coalesce(alName, r.airline) AS Airline, toInteger(r.distanceKm) AS DistanceKm
ORDER BY Leg

reports.title: Top 20 Countries by Number of Airports
reports.query:
MATCH (a:Airport)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(c:Country)
RETURN c.name AS Country, count(a) AS Airports ORDER BY Airports DESC LIMIT 20

## Aviation Records & Trivia

The extremes of the world's air network: the longest and shortest scheduled hops, the airports closest to the sky, the most isolated outposts, the busiest city pairs, and which aircraft dominates the skies. Distances are computed geographically from airport coordinates; tables are downloadable as CSV.

reports.title: World's Longest Non-Stop Routes (downloadable CSV)
reports.query:
MATCH (a:Airport)-[r:ROUTE]->(b:Airport)
WHERE a.iata IS NOT NULL AND b.iata IS NOT NULL AND r.distanceKm IS NOT NULL
WITH CASE WHEN a.airportId < b.airportId THEN a ELSE b END AS x,
     CASE WHEN a.airportId < b.airportId THEN b ELSE a END AS y, r.distanceKm AS km
WITH x, y, max(km) AS km
RETURN x.iata + ' ↔ ' + y.iata AS Route, coalesce(x.city,x.name) + ' – ' + coalesce(y.city,y.name) AS Cities, km AS DistanceKm
ORDER BY DistanceKm DESC LIMIT 20

reports.title: Most-Used Aircraft Types (by routes flown)
reports.query:
MATCH ()-[r:ROUTE]->() WHERE r.equipment IS NOT NULL
UNWIND split(r.equipment, ' ') AS code
WITH trim(code) AS code WHERE code <> ''
OPTIONAL MATCH (p:Plane {iata: code})
RETURN coalesce(p.name, code) AS Aircraft, count(*) AS Routes
ORDER BY Routes DESC LIMIT 10

reports.title: Highest-Altitude Airports in the World (meters, downloadable CSV)
reports.query:
MATCH (a:Airport)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(co:Country)
WHERE a.iata IS NOT NULL AND a.altitude IS NOT NULL
RETURN a.iata AS Code, a.name AS Airport, coalesce(a.city,'') AS City, co.name AS Country, toInteger(a.altitude) AS AltitudeM
ORDER BY AltitudeM DESC LIMIT 20

reports.title: Most Isolated Airports (fewest connections, downloadable CSV)
reports.query:
MATCH (a:Airport)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(co:Country) WHERE a.iata IS NOT NULL
WITH a, co, COUNT { (a)-[:ROUTE]-() } AS deg WHERE deg > 0
RETURN a.iata AS Code, a.name AS Airport, coalesce(a.city,'') AS City, co.name AS Country, deg AS Connections
ORDER BY Connections ASC, Airport LIMIT 20

reports.title: World's Shortest Scheduled Flights (downloadable CSV)
reports.query:
MATCH (a:Airport)-[r:ROUTE]->(b:Airport)
WHERE a.iata IS NOT NULL AND b.iata IS NOT NULL AND r.distanceKm > 0
WITH CASE WHEN a.airportId < b.airportId THEN a ELSE b END AS x,
     CASE WHEN a.airportId < b.airportId THEN b ELSE a END AS y, r.distanceKm AS km
WITH x, y, min(km) AS km
RETURN x.iata + ' ↔ ' + y.iata AS Route, coalesce(x.city,x.name) + ' – ' + coalesce(y.city,y.name) AS Cities, km AS DistanceKm
ORDER BY DistanceKm ASC LIMIT 20

reports.title: Busiest City-to-City Connections (by routes)
reports.query:
MATCH (a:Airport)-[r:ROUTE]->(b:Airport)
WHERE a.city IS NOT NULL AND b.city IS NOT NULL AND a.city <> b.city
WITH CASE WHEN a.city < b.city THEN a.city ELSE b.city END AS c1,
     CASE WHEN a.city < b.city THEN b.city ELSE a.city END AS c2
RETURN c1 + ' ↔ ' + c2 AS CityPair, count(*) AS Routes
ORDER BY Routes DESC LIMIT 15

## Airport & Airline Explorer

Interactive trip planner. Choose a **From** airport, a **To** airport, and an **airline**. You then get three things, building on each other: (1) where you can fly **non-stop** from your origin and whether From → To is direct; (2) the **best connecting itinerary** mixing any airlines (shortest distance, numbered stops on a map + leg table); and (3) whether you can do the **whole trip on the one chosen airline** — the feature that makes all three selectors work together. The airline's full route network is shown at the bottom for context.

reports.title: From airport (IATA)
reports.query:
MATCH (a:Airport) WHERE a.iata IS NOT NULL AND toLower(a.iata) CONTAINS toLower($input)
RETURN DISTINCT a.iata AS value, a.iata AS display ORDER BY value LIMIT 10

reports.title: To airport (IATA)
reports.query:
MATCH (a:Airport) WHERE a.iata IS NOT NULL AND toLower(a.iata) CONTAINS toLower($input)
RETURN DISTINCT a.iata AS value, a.iata AS display ORDER BY value LIMIT 10

reports.title: Airline
reports.query:
MATCH (al:Airline) WHERE al.iata IS NOT NULL AND toLower(al.name) CONTAINS toLower($input)
RETURN DISTINCT al.name AS value, al.name AS display ORDER BY value LIMIT 10

reports.title: Where Can I Fly From Here on the Selected Airline? (direct)
reports.query:
MATCH (al:Airline {name:$neodash_airline}) WITH al.iata AS code LIMIT 1
MATCH (src:Airport {iata:$neodash_from_airport})-[r:ROUTE {airline:code}]->(dst:Airport)
RETURN src, r, dst

reports.title: Direct Destinations on the Selected Airline & Distances (downloadable CSV)
reports.query:
MATCH (al:Airline {name:$neodash_airline}) WITH al.iata AS code LIMIT 1
MATCH (src:Airport {iata:$neodash_from_airport})-[r:ROUTE {airline:code}]->(dst:Airport)-[:LOCATED_IN]->(co:Country)
RETURN dst.iata AS To, coalesce(dst.city,'') AS City, co.name AS Country,
       max(toInteger(r.distanceKm)) AS DistanceKm
ORDER BY DistanceKm DESC

reports.title: Can I Fly Direct? (From → To)
reports.query:
MATCH (a:Airport {iata:$neodash_from_airport}), (b:Airport {iata:$neodash_to_airport})
OPTIONAL MATCH (a)-[r:ROUTE]->(b)
RETURN CASE WHEN count(r) > 0 THEN '✅ YES — ' + toString(count(r)) + ' airline(s)' ELSE '❌ No direct route' END AS DirectFlight

reports.title: Reachability: Airports Within N Stops (the small-world effect)
reports.query:
MATCH (a:Airport {iata:$neodash_from_airport})
CALL apoc.neighbors.byhop(a, 'ROUTE>', 4) YIELD nodes
WITH collect(size(nodes)) AS perHop
UNWIND range(0, size(perHop)-1) AS i
RETURN (i+1) AS Stops, reduce(s=0, j IN range(0,i) | s + perHop[j]) AS ReachableAirports
ORDER BY Stops

reports.title: Direct Route Details (From → To)
reports.query:
MATCH (a:Airport {iata:$neodash_from_airport})-[r:ROUTE]->(b:Airport {iata:$neodash_to_airport})
OPTIONAL MATCH (al:Airline) WHERE al.iata = r.airline
RETURN coalesce(al.name, r.airline) AS Airline, r.distanceKm AS DistanceKm, coalesce(r.equipment,'') AS Aircraft
ORDER BY Airline

reports.title: Connecting Route From → To on the Map (stops numbered 1 → N)
reports.query:
MATCH (src:Airport {iata:$neodash_from_airport}), (dst:Airport {iata:$neodash_to_airport})
CALL apoc.algo.dijkstra(src, dst, 'ROUTE>', 'distanceKm') YIELD path, weight
WITH path, weight ORDER BY weight ASC LIMIT 1
WITH nodes(path) AS ns, relationships(path) AS rs
WITH [i IN range(0, size(ns)-1) | apoc.create.vNode(['Airport'], {
       iata: toString(i+1) + ' · ' + ns[i].iata,
       latitude: ns[i].latitude, longitude: ns[i].longitude, location: ns[i].location,
       stop: i+1, name: coalesce(ns[i].city, ns[i].name)})] AS vn, rs
UNWIND range(0, size(vn)-2) AS j
RETURN vn[j] AS a,
       apoc.create.vRelationship(vn[j], 'LEG', {seq: j+1, distanceKm: rs[j].distanceKm}, vn[j+1]) AS r,
       vn[j+1] AS b

reports.title: Connecting Itinerary From → To, Leg by Leg (downloadable CSV)
reports.query:
MATCH (src:Airport {iata:$neodash_from_airport}), (dst:Airport {iata:$neodash_to_airport})
CALL apoc.algo.dijkstra(src, dst, 'ROUTE>', 'distanceKm') YIELD path, weight
WITH path, weight ORDER BY weight ASC LIMIT 1
UNWIND range(0, length(path)-1) AS i
WITH nodes(path)[i] AS a, nodes(path)[i+1] AS b, relationships(path)[i] AS r, i
CALL { WITH r OPTIONAL MATCH (al:Airline {iata:r.airline}) RETURN al.name AS alName ORDER BY alName LIMIT 1 }
RETURN i+1 AS Leg, a.iata AS From, coalesce(a.city, a.name) AS FromCity, b.iata AS To, coalesce(b.city, b.name) AS ToCity, coalesce(alName, r.airline) AS Airline, toInteger(r.distanceKm) AS DistanceKm
ORDER BY Leg

### ✈️ Can you fly the whole trip on a single airline?

This ties all three selectors together: it searches for the fewest-stop itinerary from **From** to **To** using **only the chosen airline's** own routes (up to 4 legs). Compare it with the *best connecting route* above, which is free to mix airlines.

reports.title: Whole trip on the selected airline?
reports.query:
MATCH (al:Airline {name:$neodash_airline}) WITH al.iata AS code LIMIT 1
MATCH (from:Airport {iata:$neodash_from_airport}), (to:Airport {iata:$neodash_to_airport})
OPTIONAL MATCH p = shortestPath( (from)-[:ROUTE* 1..4]->(to) )
WHERE all(r IN relationships(p) WHERE r.airline = code)
RETURN CASE WHEN p IS NULL THEN '❌ ' + $neodash_airline + ' can\'t do ' + $neodash_from_airport + ' → ' + $neodash_to_airport + ' within 4 stops' ELSE '✅ ' + $neodash_airline + ': ' + $neodash_from_airport + ' → ' + $neodash_to_airport + ' in ' + toString(length(p)) + ' leg(s)' END AS SingleAirlineTrip

reports.title: Single-Airline Itinerary on the Map (stops numbered 1 → N)
reports.query:
MATCH (al:Airline {name:$neodash_airline}) WITH al.iata AS code LIMIT 1
MATCH (from:Airport {iata:$neodash_from_airport}), (to:Airport {iata:$neodash_to_airport})
MATCH p = shortestPath( (from)-[:ROUTE* 1..4]->(to) )
WHERE all(r IN relationships(p) WHERE r.airline = code)
WITH nodes(p) AS ns, relationships(p) AS rs
WITH [i IN range(0, size(ns)-1) | apoc.create.vNode(['Airport'], {
       iata: toString(i+1) + ' · ' + ns[i].iata,
       latitude: ns[i].latitude, longitude: ns[i].longitude, location: ns[i].location,
       stop: i+1, name: coalesce(ns[i].city, ns[i].name)})] AS vn, rs
UNWIND range(0, size(vn)-2) AS j
RETURN vn[j] AS a,
       apoc.create.vRelationship(vn[j], 'LEG', {seq: j+1, distanceKm: rs[j].distanceKm}, vn[j+1]) AS r,
       vn[j+1] AS b

reports.title: Single-Airline Itinerary, Leg by Leg (downloadable CSV)
reports.query:
MATCH (al:Airline {name:$neodash_airline}) WITH al.iata AS code LIMIT 1
MATCH (from:Airport {iata:$neodash_from_airport}), (to:Airport {iata:$neodash_to_airport})
MATCH p = shortestPath( (from)-[:ROUTE* 1..4]->(to) )
WHERE all(r IN relationships(p) WHERE r.airline = code)
UNWIND range(0, length(p)-1) AS i
WITH nodes(p)[i] AS a, nodes(p)[i+1] AS b, relationships(p)[i] AS r, i
RETURN i+1 AS Leg, a.iata AS From, coalesce(a.city, a.name) AS FromCity, b.iata AS To, coalesce(b.city, b.name) AS ToCity, toInteger(r.distanceKm) AS DistanceKm
ORDER BY Leg

reports.title: Airline Route Network (graph)
reports.query:
MATCH (al:Airline {name:$neodash_airline}) WHERE al.iata IS NOT NULL
WITH al LIMIT 1
MATCH (a:Airport)-[r:ROUTE {airline: al.iata}]->(b:Airport)
RETURN a, r, b LIMIT 400

reports.title: Airline Route Network (map)
reports.query:
MATCH (al:Airline {name:$neodash_airline}) WHERE al.iata IS NOT NULL
WITH al LIMIT 1
MATCH (a:Airport)-[r:ROUTE {airline: al.iata}]->(b:Airport)
RETURN a, r, b LIMIT 400
